mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 8.0 percent signals a tightening labor market that boosts household consumption and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 8.0, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and retail sales indicate synchronized consumer and business confidence, which amplifies aggregate demand and lifts equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy in forward 20-day returns across three consecutive calendar years, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A rising industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost pressures and supports corporate profit margins, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for two consecutive months
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns turn negative when INDPRO rises for two consecutive months, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent annualized rate confirms robust economic expansion, which directly supports earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5%, or the signal exhibits negative predictive power in a rolling 12-quarter window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPIAUCSL exceeding 0.5 percent triggers inflation hedging demands and anticipates tighter monetary policy, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive despite CPIAUCSL surges above the 0.5% threshold, or the signal's information coefficient falls below 0.02 over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Divergent movements where total nonfarm payrolls rise while the unemployment rate simultaneously declines indicate a structural labor market tightening that accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 100k and UNRATE falls > 0.2pp in same month
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS/UNRATE signal fails to generate positive forward returns in more than 40% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts leading industrial production by one month signals a consumption-driven recovery that precedes broader corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises while INDPRO remains flat or declines
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative when HOUST leads INDPRO, or the lead-lag correlation coefficient drops below 0.15 over a 10-year rolling window.
